scope: United States Chevrolet fleet, government, Special Equipment Option, and other specialty exterior colors, plus qualified historical exterior-color table subsets. Forest Service Green is retained as a named USDA research lead without an invented Chevrolet model-year application.

required_fields: exact Chevrolet model or model variant
manufacturer-printed paint, option, or SEO code
publication_policy.mutable_source_rule: A mutable API response is not permanent evidence unless the complete response bytes, retrieval timestamp, and SHA-256 digest are retained. Complete generated GM Order Guide PDFs satisfy this rule for the captured snapshot.
publication_policy.identity_rule: Similar green labels and repeated SEO codes are not treated as the same paint identity without an explicit primary-source bridge.

promoted_pdf_pages_visually_rechecked: gm-2013-municipal-guide PDF pages 80, 92, 136, 151, and Silverado section 164-180
integrity_audit.publication_boundary: The 533 app_publication_records are limited to directly reviewed model, year, and program scopes. The 1981 Woodland Green records are limited to four separately reviewed Sportvan, Chevy Van, Cutaway/Hi-Cube, and Step-Van/Step-Van King charts, preserve ZY1, ZY2, code 02, and bare-aluminum distinctions, and assert no factory-installation claim or continuity with SEO 9V5. The 1983 permanent fleet rows are limited to the C/K Pickup, preserve the Cardinal Red S-10/15 and El Camino/Caballero exclusion, print no inferred factory paint code or finish, and assert no assembly-plant installation. The 1993 C/K Pickup tranche is limited to the four zero-lead-day solid paints on PDF page 12 and does not assert assembly-plant installation or extend them to another model. The Caprice PPV tranche contains one row per exact 2011-2017 program, color, and model year, applies the revised model-specific guide over conflicting Fleet Guide summaries, and publishes no inferred WA, SEO, or factory paint code. The 2024 Blazer EV SEO rows marked not available and the 2025 rows marked planned or not available remain nonrouting snapshot evidence. The five no-RPO rows on the 2003 SEO chart remain research-only because the adjacent TGK ordering table expressly identifies only four orderable specialty colors. Forest Service Green remains a research lead without Chevrolet model-year availability. The 2012-2014 municipal SEO tranche publishes 132 exact program-color rows, preserves blank versus literal TBD code cells, records the 2014 Tahoe and Suburban deletions, and does not infer a 2013 Silverado specialty palette. The 2015-2020 later-fleet specialty tranche publishes 112 exact program-color rows from eleven separately retained and visually reviewed GM Upfitter guides, keeps Tahoe 9C1 and 5W4 separate, preserves Impala Kerr post-build application semantics, and does not infer adjacent years or route Forest Service Green.

structured_field_backfills.basis: The source table states that these special equipment options were installed at the assembly plant.

program_evolution: 9C3 is documented for 2011 through 2013 and expressly deleted for 2014.
Hugo Blue is batch restricted for 2012 through 2016.
Hugo Blue and Mystic Green are expressly discontinued for 2017.

The reviewed 5W4 years are exactly 2015, 2016, 2019, and 2020.
No 2017 or 2018 5W4 palette is inferred from an adjacent year.
The 2015 and 2016 tables print no five-order minimum; the 2019 and 2020 tables do.

app_publication_records.program_label: Impala 9C1 and 9C3 Kerr Industries authorized-upfitter special-paint program
app_publication_records.label: Adriatic Blue
app_publication_records.finish: authorized upfitter solid or two-tone application
app_publication_records.code_display: WA# 121A; Code 1 BEA; Code 2 BFE; AAS solid or AAT two-tone
restrictions: This is a Kerr Industries authorized-upfitter program, not evidence that General Motors applied the finish at the assembly plant.
Orders were sent to Kerr Industries after the cars were built and incurred an additional charge.
RPO White 50U or RPO Black 41U had to be ordered; Black 41U was recommended before special paint was applied.
For paint schemes, only Class A surfaces were painted unless the dealer separately arranged and paid Kerr to paint mirrors and handles.
Special-paint warranty claims were directed to Kerr Industries.
The source table heading is WA#, but the cells print the four-character value without a WA- prefix.

app_publication_records.program_label: Impala Limited 9C1 and 9C3 Kerr Industries authorized-upfitter special-paint program
app_publication_records.label: Olive
app_publication_records.finish: authorized upfitter solid or two-tone application
app_publication_records.code_display: WA# 311B; Code 1 BEB; Code 2 BFF; AAS solid or AAT two-tone
restrictions: This is a Kerr Industries authorized-upfitter program, not evidence that General Motors applied the finish at the assembly plant.
Orders were sent to Kerr Industries after the cars were built and incurred an additional charge.
RPO White 50U or RPO Black 41U had to be ordered; Black 41U was recommended before special paint was applied.
For paint schemes, only Class A surfaces were painted unless the dealer separately arranged and paid Kerr to paint mirrors and handles.
Special-paint warranty claims were directed to Kerr Industries.
The source table heading is WA#, but the cells print the four-character value without a WA- prefix.

app_publication_records.code_display: WA# 9403; Code 1 BEO; Code 2 BFS; AAS solid or AAT two-tone
restrictions: This is a Kerr Industries authorized-upfitter program, not evidence that General Motors applied the finish at the assembly plant.
Orders were sent to Kerr Industries after the cars were built and incurred an additional charge.
RPO White 50U or RPO Black 41U had to be ordered; Black 41U was recommended before special paint was applied.
For paint schemes, only Class A surfaces were painted unless the dealer separately arranged and paid Kerr to paint mirrors and handles.
Special-paint warranty claims were directed to Kerr Industries.
The source table heading is WA#, but the cells print the four-character value without a WA- prefix.

source.title: 2014 Chevrolet Municipal Vehicles Technical Manual
source.section: Impala Limited 9C1 and 9C3 - Special Paint Available; Paint Schemes

app_publication_records.program_label: 1981 Sportvan Exterior Color Availability Chart
app_publication_records.label: Woodland Green
restrictions: The Sportvan chart prints Woodland Green as primary color 46 and secondary color 46 for solid paint.
The chart also prints Woodland Green primary 46 with Frost White secondary 12 or Light Silver Metallic secondary 17 as recommended two-tone combinations.
The chart states that any exterior combination may be ordered with any available seat trim.
This record is limited to Sportvan. Chevy Van, Cutaway Van, Hi-Cube Van, Step-Van, and adjacent years are not inferred.
The source does not state an assembly-plant installation claim.

source.title: 1981 Chevrolet G Van Vehicle Information Kit
source.section: Sportvan Interior and Exterior Color Availability Chart

app_publication_records.program_label: 1981 Chevy Van Exterior Color Availability Chart
app_publication_records.label: Woodland Green
restrictions: The Chevy Van chart prints Woodland Green as primary color 46 and secondary color 46 for solid paint.
The chart also prints Woodland Green primary 46 with Frost White secondary 12 or Light Silver Metallic secondary 17 as recommended two-tone combinations.
The chart states that any exterior combination may be ordered with any available seat trim.
This record is limited to Chevy Van. The adjacent Nomad and Beauville seat-trim footnote is not represented as a Woodland Green restriction.
Sportvan, Cutaway Van, Hi-Cube Van, Step-Van, and adjacent years are not inferred.
The source does not state an assembly-plant installation claim.

source.title: 1981 Chevrolet G Van Vehicle Information Kit
source.section: Chevy Van Interior and Exterior Color Availability Chart

app_publication_records.program_label: 1981 Cutaway Van and Hi-Cube Van Exterior Color Availability Chart
app_publication_records.label: Woodland Green
app_publication_records.code_display: 46; ZY1 solid; ZY2 aluminum-body Hi-Cube two-tone with bare aluminum 02
restrictions: The Cutaway Van and Hi-Cube Van chart prints Woodland Green as primary color 46 and secondary color 46.
Cutaway Van and steel-body Hi-Cube Van may be ordered only with ZY1 solid paint.
Aluminum-body Hi-Cube Van may use ZY1 solid paint or ZY2 two-tone paint.
For ZY2 on an aluminum-body Hi-Cube Van, the cab is primary color 46 and the body is unpainted bare aluminum, secondary code 02.
Code 02 denotes bare aluminum, not Woodland Green.
Sportvan, Chevy Van, Step-Van, and adjacent years are not inferred.
The source does not state an assembly-plant installation claim.

source.title: 1981 Chevrolet Motorhome Vehicle Information Kit
source.section: Cutaway Van and Hi-Cube Van Interior and Exterior Color Availability Chart

app_publication_records.program_label: 1981 Step-Van and Step-Van King Exterior Color Availability Chart
app_publication_records.label: Woodland Green

paint_systems.relationship_to_woodland_green: separate alternative, not a Woodland Green code

restrictions: The Step-Van chart prints Woodland Green as primary color 46 and secondary color 46.
Two-tone paint is not available for Step-Van or Step-Van King.
ZY1 solid paint is standard on the steel E32 body-code Step-Van and available on the aluminum E33 body-code Step-Van.
Bare aluminum code 02 is available only on the aluminum E33 Step-Van and is a separate alternative, not a Woodland Green code.
Sportvan, Chevy Van, Cutaway Van, Hi-Cube Van, and adjacent years are not inferred.
The source does not state an assembly-plant installation claim.

rejected_or_unresolved_leads.lead: A Wikimedia Commons image caption identifies a 2002 Chevrolet Silverado K2500HD as Forest Service Green.
rejected_or_unresolved_leads.reason: An uploader caption and photograph do not establish factory paint, original finish, exact GM code, restriction, or orderability.

rejected_or_unresolved_leads.lead: 2002 Chevrolet Silverado and Silverado HD official vehicle information kits
rejected_or_unresolved_leads.reason: The complete scanned kits were reviewed as source candidates, but no page located in this pass tied Forest Service Green to a Chevrolet model, year, or GM code.
Searches of the official GM, GM Upfitter, and USDA source sets found no manufacturer or agency paint label exactly named Forestry Green.

rejected_or_unresolved_leads.lead: Equating historical Woodland Green option 46 / SEO 9V5 with modern Woodland Green WA-9015 / 9V5
rejected_or_unresolved_leads.reason: The repeated label and SEO code are leads, not an explicit primary-source identity bridge.

rejected_or_unresolved_leads.lead: Equating USDA identifier 5032 with Federal Standard No. 595 color 14260
rejected_or_unresolved_leads.reason: Separate official USDA specifications print each identifier, but no reviewed primary source explicitly states that they are the same standard color.

source_precedence_policies.higher_authority: The revised, model-specific General Motors municipal or Caprice specification guide.
source_precedence_policies.lower_authority: The annual General Motors Fleet Guide summary when its label, omission, or carryover conflicts with the model-specific guide.
source_precedence_policies.rule: Publish the final palette in the model-specific guide. Preserve every conflicting Fleet Guide literal in source_conflict_assertions without creating an additional availability row.

source_conflict_assertions.resolution: The app rows preserve the model-specific 9C1 and 9C3 labels. Fleet labels remain comparison literals only.
Hugo Blue is published with its 20-unit batch restriction; the model-guide GST and GGG literals control.

differences.model_guide: Deleted and omitted from both final palette tables

source_conflict_assertions.resolution: No 2013 GST availability record is published.
GZ7 is published once as Mystic Green. Prussian Steel remains a source-literal alias, not a second color.

differences.model_guide: Omitted from the revised final palette

source_conflict_assertions.resolution: No 2015 GGG availability record is published because the 2014 model guide deleted it and the revised 2015 palette omits it.

differences.model_guide: Discontinued and omitted from the final palette

source_conflict_assertions.resolution: No 2017 GZ7 availability record is published.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: RPO GST
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The 9C3 update page marks Mirage Gold Metallic deleted; both final Caprice palette tables omit GST.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: Caprice Detective Package 9C3
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The guide lists the Detective Package, option 9C3, under deletions.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2014 or later Caprice 9C3 palette is published.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: RPO GGG Alto Grey Metallic
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The guide lists Alto Grey Metallic under deletions and omits GGG from the final palette.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2014 or later GGG row is published.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: RPO GZ7 Mystic Green
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The guide identifies Mystic Green as a new exterior color and includes it in the final palette.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: RPO GYW Hugo Blue
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The update page lists Hugo Blue as discontinued and the final palette omits it.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: RPO GZ7 Mystic Green
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The update page lists Mystic Green as discontinued and the final palette omits it.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2014 Tahoe 2WD Police Package PPV SEO palette
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The 2014 update page expressly lists these seven specialty paints under Deleted; the final SEO table prints only Victory Red WA-9260, SEO 5T4.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2014 availability row is published for a deleted color. Victory Red remains a separately source-confirmed active row.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2014 Tahoe 4WD Special Service 5W4 SEO palette
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The 2014 update page expressly lists these seven specialty paints under Deleted; the final SEO table prints only Victory Red WA-9260, SEO 5T4.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2014 availability row is published for a deleted color. Victory Red remains a separately source-confirmed active row.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2014 Suburban Commercial Fleet 1FL SEO palette
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The 2014 update page expressly lists these seven specialty paints under Deleted; the final SEO table prints only Victory Red WA-9260, SEO 5T4.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2014 availability row is published for a deleted color. Victory Red remains a separately source-confirmed active row.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2014 Suburban 3/4-ton models
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The 2014 Suburban 1FL update page states that there were no 3/4-ton models for the 2014 model year.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: The 2014 1FL SEO row is not extended to a 3/4-ton Suburban configuration.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2013 Silverado 1500 Crew Cab Pickup Work Truck 1WT SEO paint
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The complete 2013 Silverado 1WT section on PDF pages 164-180 prints a standard Available Exterior Colors block on PDF page 168 but no SEO Paint Available table.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No 2013 Silverado specialty-paint row is published. Silence is not treated as affirmative unavailability, and neither 2012 nor 2014 is propagated into 2013.

program_lifecycle_assertions.subject: 2018 Tahoe 9C1 Havana Brown Metallic standard-palette row
program_lifecycle_assertions.fact: The standard exterior-color block marks Havana Brown Metallic as no longer available to order as of 11-20-17; it is not a row in the SEO Paint Available table.
program_lifecycle_assertions.publication_effect: No Havana Brown Metallic specialty-availability row is published, and the standard-palette lifecycle note is not promoted into the SEO table.

premium_color_restrictions.effect: Orders below 20 units are delayed until a 20-unit production batch is received; additional lead time is required.
Orders below 20 units are delayed until a 20-unit production batch is received; additional lead time is required.
